tasks.description: Add `resolveGroupTrackId(group, rows)` and `findGroupForTrack(groups, shotId, trackId, rows)` helpers to `src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-projection.ts`. resolveGroupTrackId: fast path checks stored trackId row for matching clipIds (O(1) row + O(clips)); slow path scans all rows (O(rows×clips)); falls back to stored trackId if no clips found. findGroupForTrack: exact (shotId, trackId) match first, then fallback to resolveGroupTrackId scan. Export both alongside existing exports.

Add tests for both new helpers in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-projection.test.ts`. Follow existing test patterns (vitest, buildConfig factory, V1/V2 tracks). Test cases for resolveGroupTrackId: (a) clips on stored track (fast path), (b) clips moved to different track (slow path), (c) no live clips (orphaned — returns stored trackId), (d) clips split across multiple tracks (returns track with most). Test cases for findGroupForTrack: (a) exact match, (b) stale trackId found via resolution, (c) no match returns undefined, (d) multiple groups for same shot on different tracks.

Fix 6 lookup sites in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/shot-group-commands.ts` to use findGroupForTrack/resolveGroupTrackId. Sites: (1) buildPinnedShotGroupsOverride line ~69 existingIndex, (2) buildUnpinShotGroupMutation line ~106 filter, (3) buildUpdatePinnedShotGroupMutation line ~123 map, (4) buildDeleteShotGroupMutation line ~161 filter, (5) buildSwitchShotGroupToFinalVideoMutation line ~229 find, (6) buildUpdateShotGroupToLatestVideoMutation line ~347 and buildSwitchShotGroupToImagesMutation line ~398. All these functions receive `currentData: TimelineData` which has `rows`. Self-heal: when a group is found with stale trackId, include the corrected trackId in the output mutation arrays.

Add stale-trackId test cases to `src/tools/video-editor/lib/shot-group-commands.test.ts`. For each mutation builder, add a test where group says trackId='V1' but clips are actually on 'V2', verifying: (a) the builder still finds and operates on the group, (b) the output mutation self-heals trackId to 'V2'. Read existing tests first to understand fixture patterns.

Fix the sync loop in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePinnedShotGroups.ts`. CRITICAL: separate storedTrackId (for identity matching against workingPinnedShotGroups candidates at lines ~365, 370-371) from resolvedTrackId (for all row/data access at lines ~198, 204, 213, 218-219, 242, 278, 317, 357-359). At top of each loop iteration: `const resolvedTrackId = resolveGroupTrackId(group, workingData.rows); const storedTrackId = group.trackId;`. Use resolvedTrackId for 8 row-lookup/clipOrder/appendActionToTrack/buildAssetDropEdit references. Use storedTrackId for identity-matching comparisons. Self-heal in nextGroups output: when resolvedTrackId !== storedTrackId, spread resolved trackId into the matched candidate. Update the filter at ~369-372 to also match by storedTrackId.

Fix display hook `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts`. At line ~39, use resolveGroupTrackId instead of raw group.trackId for the rowIndex lookup. At line ~66, use resolvedTrackId for the output ShotGroup.rowId. This ensures shot group overlays render on the correct track when trackId is stale.

Fix 4 vulnerable sites in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts`. (1) moveClipToRow line ~210: resolve enclosingGroup.group.trackId via resolveGroupTrackId before finding sourceTrack. (2) translateGroupMembers line ~105: reads group.trackId as sourceTrackId — callers must pass resolved group (verify caller fix at moveClipToRow covers this). (3) createTrackAndMoveClip line ~336: resolve enclosingGroup?.group.trackId. (4) moveSelectedClipsToTrack lines ~498-501, 506-507, 618-621: use findGroupForTrack for group lookups, resolvedTrackId for row lookups and output rowId.

Fix 3 lookup sites in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts`. (1) buildRestoreShotGroupVideoMutation lines ~78-80: use findGroupForTrack to find the video group. (2) Same function line ~104: use findGroupForTrack for the map update pattern. (3) updateToLatestVideo callback lines ~164-166: use findGroupForTrack with dataRef.current.rows. These functions receive TimelineData which has rows.

Fix drag session origin in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.helpers.ts` line ~285. Replace `originTrackId: enclosingGroup.group.trackId` with `originTrackId: resolveGroupTrackId(enclosingGroup.group, current.rows)`. The function already has `current` (TimelineData) available. This prevents planMultiDragMoves from aborting when the stale track has been removed by handleClearUnusedTracks. No changes needed in multi-drag-utils.ts itself — the existing fix at lines 224-246 handles the rest.

Run ALL video editor tests and TypeScript compilation. (1) Run `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-projection.test.ts` — new helper tests. (2) Run `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/lib/shot-group-commands.test.ts` — command tests. (3) Run `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/lib/multi-drag-utils.test.ts` — drag tests (regression). (4) Run `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts` — display tests. (5) Run `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts` — track mgmt tests. (6) Run `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.softtag.test.tsx` — drag session tests. (7) Run `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/` — full video editor suite. (8) Run `npx tsc --noEmit` — TypeScript compilation check. Fix any failures, re-run until clean.

Run tests relevant to the changed files to verify correctness and check for regressions — run the full test file/module, not just individual functions. Find and run the project's existing test suite — do NOT create new test files. If any test fails, read the error, fix the code, and re-run until all tests pass.

watch_items: FLAG-001 (addressed): The sync loop in usePinnedShotGroups MUST separate storedTrackId (identity matching at lines ~365, 370-371) from resolvedTrackId (row/data access). Mixing them up causes the self-heal to break the map/filter — the updated group won't match any candidate.

FLAG-002 (open, minor): useShotGroupHandlers.ts:73-98 builds staleShotGroupIds from stored group.trackId as `${shotId}:${trackId}`. TimelineCanvas checks `${shotId}:${group.rowId}`. This file is NOT in scope for this plan — acknowledged as remaining minor debt. Do not accidentally break it further.

FLAG-003 (addressed): buildPendingDragSession at useClipDrag.helpers.ts:285 must resolve originTrackId proactively. If the old track was removed by handleClearUnusedTracks, planMultiDragMoves would abort with canMove:false.
Groups are keyed by (shotId, trackId) composite — the same shot CAN be pinned on multiple tracks. Helpers must not collapse to shotId-only matching.
resolveGroupTrackId fast path MUST be O(1) row lookup + O(clips) membership check. Only scan all rows on miss. Groups are small (3-10 clips), timelines have 3-8 rows.
translateGroupMembers at useTimelineTrackManagement.ts:155-158 is the ONE existing place that correctly updates trackId. Ensure the new helpers don't conflict with this existing self-healing path.
Self-healing must be convergent: after any operation touches a stale group, the stored trackId should match reality in the output mutation.
The plan explicitly does NOT create new files for helpers — resolveGroupTrackId and findGroupForTrack go in the existing pinned-group-projection.ts.
shot-group-commands.ts functions are pure (receive currentData, return mutations). Self-healing means including corrected trackId in the returned pinnedShotGroupsOverride arrays.
Debt watch: do not make pinned-group-track-sync debt worse — this plan is specifically fixing it. Verify all 26 vulnerable sites are covered.

sense_checks.question: Do resolveGroupTrackId and findGroupForTrack correctly handle the multi-track-per-shot case? Specifically: if shot-1 is pinned on V1 and V2, does findGroupForTrack(groups, 'shot-1', 'V1', rows) return only the V1 group even when V1's trackId is stale?
Do the new tests include a case where clips are split across multiple rows (e.g., 2 clips on V1, 3 on V2) and verify that resolveGroupTrackId returns the row with the most clips?

In each mutation builder, when a stale group is found via resolution, does the output pinnedShotGroupsOverride array contain the corrected trackId? Check that the self-heal propagates through the returned data, not just the local variable.
Do the stale-trackId test cases verify both that the operation succeeds AND that trackId is self-healed in the output? A test that only checks 'group was found' misses the self-healing requirement.

In the sync loop: are ALL 8 row-lookup/clipOrder/appendActionToTrack references using resolvedTrackId, while the 2 identity-matching comparisons at lines ~365 and 370-371 use storedTrackId? And does the nextGroups output spread resolvedTrackId when stale?
Does the ShotGroup output at line ~66 use resolvedTrackId for rowId, ensuring downstream consumers (including TimelineCanvas overlay rendering) see the correct track?
In moveSelectedClipsToTrack, are the group lookups at lines ~498-501 and ~618-621 both using findGroupForTrack? And does moveClipToRow pass a group with resolved trackId to translateGroupMembers?
Do all three useSwitchToFinalVideo lookup sites now use findGroupForTrack, and do they pass rows from the available TimelineData?
Does buildPendingDragSession now call resolveGroupTrackId with current.rows, and is the resolved value stored as originTrackId in the drag session?

Do ALL existing tests pass without modification (except tests updated for new signatures)? Does `npx tsc --noEmit` complete with zero errors? Were any test failures investigated and fixed rather than skipped?
Did the verification tests pass? Were any regressions found and fixed?

meta_commentary:
Execution guidance:

1. **Start with T1 (helpers)**. These are pure functions with no side effects — easy to get right and test. The key design decisions: resolveGroupTrackId must check the stored-trackId row FIRST (fast path) before scanning all rows. Use a Set of clipIds for O(1) membership checks. Return the row with the most matching clips when split across rows. Fall back to stored trackId (not undefined) for orphaned groups.

2. **T3/T5/T7 are the highest-risk tasks.** T5 (sync loop) is the most complex — carefully count all 8 row-access references vs 2 identity-matching references. Read the actual current code before editing; line numbers in the plan may have drifted slightly from recent commits.

3. **T3-T4 and T5-T9 can be parallelized** since they touch different files. But T5 deserves the most careful review.

4. **Self-healing pattern**: When any function finds a group via resolution (meaning resolvedTrackId != storedTrackId), the OUTPUT mutation must include `trackId: resolvedTrackId` in the group object. This is how staleness gets repaired on contact.

5. **Don't touch useShotGroupHandlers.ts** (FLAG-002). It's a minor issue where stale-video badges use `group.trackId` — but fixing it via the useShotGroups.ts display hook (T6) partially addresses this since ShotGroup.rowId will now be correct. The staleShotGroupIds set in useShotGroupHandlers still uses raw group.trackId, but fixing that is out of scope per the plan.

6. **Import paths**: Both new helpers import from the same file they're defined in, so no circular dependency risk. Callers in hooks/ and lib/ already import from pinned-group-projection.ts.

7. **Type signature for resolveGroupTrackId**: `(group: PinnedShotGroup, rows: TimelineRow[]) => string`. Always returns a string (falls back to group.trackId). For findGroupForTrack: `(groups: PinnedShotGroup[], shotId: string, trackId: string, rows: TimelineRow[]) => PinnedShotGroup | undefined`.

8. **The categorizeSelection function** in pinned-group-projection.ts returns `PinnedGroupKey` objects with `{ shotId, trackId }`. These keys come from stored group state and may be stale. The plan addresses this in T7 (moveSelectedClipsToTrack) by using findGroupForTrack to look up the actual group from a potentially-stale key.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Add resolveGroupTrackId and findGroupForTrack helpers to pinned-group-projection.ts
Step 2: Add tests for the new helpers in pinned-group-projection.test.ts
Step 3: Fix 6 lookups in shot-group-commands.ts with findGroupForTrack and self-healing
Step 4: Add stale-trackId test cases to shot-group-commands.test.ts
Step 5: Fix the sync loop in usePinnedShotGroups.ts (storedTrackId vs resolvedTrackId separation)
Step 6: Fix display hook useShotGroups.ts line 39 and 66
Step 7: Fix 4 vulnerable sites in useTimelineTrackManagement.ts
Step 8: Fix 3 lookup sites in useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts
Step 9: Fix drag session origin in useClipDrag.helpers.ts and multi-drag-utils.ts
Step 10: Run existing tests for each modified file
Step 11: Run full video editor test suite and TypeScript check

validation.completeness_notes: All 11 plan steps are covered. Steps 10 and 11 are combined into T10 since they are both validation steps that should run together. The plan's Phase 1 (helpers), Phase 2 (commands), Phase 3 (hooks), and Phase 4 (validation) are all represented. FLAG-002 (useShotGroupHandlers stale-video badge) is acknowledged as out-of-scope minor debt per the plan.

baseline_test_note: Baseline tests could not be run during finalization due to tool approval constraints. The executor MUST run the full video editor test suite (`npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/`) before making any changes to establish the baseline. Any pre-existing failures should be noted and excluded from the regression check.
